qa_pairs.question: What additional options should be used with `apptainer run` on Alliance clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: On our clusters, you will want to use a number of additional options, including `-C`, `-c`, `-e`, `-W`, as well as various bind mount options to make your disk space available to the programs that run in your container.

qa_pairs.question: Can you provide an example of using `apptainer run` to compile a C++ program on an Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: An example command to compile a C++ program on an Alliance cluster is: `apptainer run -C -W $SLURM_TMPDIR -B /project -B /scratch your-container-name.sif g++ -O2 -march=broadwell ./myprog.cpp`.

qa_pairs.question: How do I run commands interactively within an Apptainer container?
qa_pairs.answer: To run commands interactively while remaining inside a container, use the `apptainer shell` command instead.

qa_pairs.question: What is the basic command to start an interactive shell for an Apptainer container?
qa_pairs.answer: To run commands interactively in a container, one first invokes the `apptainer shell` command, e.g., `apptainer shell your-container-name.sif`.

qa_pairs.question: What prompt will I see when I'm inside an interactive Apptainer shell session?
qa_pairs.answer: Once the container starts, you will see an `Apptainer>` prompt (or `Singularity>` prompt if using Singularity).

qa_pairs.question: How do I exit an interactive Apptainer shell session?
qa_pairs.answer: When done, type `exit` and hit the Enter/Return key to exit the container.

qa_pairs.question: What additional options should be used with `apptainer shell` on Alliance clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: On our clusters, you will want to use a number of additional options, including `-C`, `-c`, `-e`, `-W`, as well as various bind mount options to make your disk space available to the programs that run in your container.

qa_pairs.question: Can you give an example of using `apptainer shell` with recommended options on an Alliance cluster?
What is crucial for saving changes to a persistent overlay image during an `apptainer shell` session?
qa_pairs.answer: If you are making use of a persistent overlay image and want changes to be written to that image, it is extremely important to pass the `-w` or `--writable` option to your container. If this option is not passed to it, any changes you make to the image in the `apptainer shell` session will not be saved!

qa_pairs.question: How does Apptainer facilitate running daemons within compute jobs on clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Apptainer has been designed to be able to properly run daemons within compute jobs on clusters, and running daemons is achieved, in part, by using `apptainer instance`.

qa_pairs.question: Why should I use `apptainer instance` instead of manually running daemons?
qa_pairs.answer: `apptainer instance` works properly with other tools such as the Slurm scheduler, preventing daemons from hanging or becoming defunct processes if a job is cancelled, killed, crashes, or otherwise finished. Additionally, it allows you to control the daemons and programs running in the same container.

qa_pairs.question: What is the lifespan of daemons run using `apptainer instance` on Alliance clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Daemons can only run in your job while your job is running; should the scheduler kill your job, all daemons running in that job will also be killed.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I need to run daemons longer than the duration of my compute job?
qa_pairs.answer: Should you need to run daemons longer than the job they were started in, you may need to also have a virtual machine running in the cloud; if so, please contact technical support.
